displayTitle: The Source Rides in the Truck
kicker: RAD SAFETY // EXPOSURE CONTROL IN DAILY OPERATIONS

beats.narration: Everything you've learned comes together on the job, where a source that can deliver a lethal dose rides in the truck all day.

payload.title: NAME THE HAZARD HONESTLY
payload.sub: The largest source you'll ever stand beside.

beats.narration: The danger concentrates where the source leaves its shield: out the guide tube to the collimator, hundreds of R per hour within a foot.

payload.heading: Where people actually get hurt
payload.note: GUIDE TUBE + SOURCE STOP = THE DANGER ZONE

beats.narration: So control it in order of strength. Protections that work automatically beat the ones that depend on a tired worker remembering.

chips.s: Built in, always on, no decision
Inverse-square is your strongest field tool
Detect and warn; the layer that tires

beats.narration: Only the meter knows where the source is, so a survey brackets every shot. Set the boundary by measured dose rate, not a tape.

tiles.s: Set the boundary by dose rate

beats.narration: Most serious accidents aren't exotic: a disconnected source, a skipped survey, a dead meter, caught too late. After every exposure, retract and confirm.

payload.sub: The handle can read home while the source hangs up. The meter cannot lie.
